top_stories.title: Philippine banks’ bad loans climb to 6-month high in February
top_stories.summary: Philippine banks’ bad loan ratio climbed to 3.33 percent in February, marking its highest level in six months—a level that remains manageable but still warrants closer monitoring if higher interest rates persist. According to the latest Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) data, the banking industry’s non-performing lo...
top_stories.why_it_matters: This matters because policy pressure can change how banks lend, preserve liquidity, price risk, or compete for deposits.

top_stories.title: BSP stays cautious, sees policy tightening only if inflation broadens
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines – Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) Governor Eli Remolona Jr. said the central bank was adopting a wait-and-see stance amid the global energy shock, stressing that it would tighten policy only if supply-driven price pressures begin to feed into demand. In an interview with CNBC, Remolona said the...
top_stories.why_it_matters: This belongs in the top group because it points to a live shift in banking-system behavior, not just a company headline.

top_stories.title: SEC pushes overhaul of online lending rules
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has received broad support for its plan to lift the moratorium on online lending platforms while tightening capital requirements, as regulators move to weed out abusive players in the fast-growing sector. top_stories.title: BSP readies revisions to rules on salary loan terms
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) has asked lenders to remove the repayment term limit for salary loans to give retail borrowers, including teachers, more flexibility in managing debt. The central bank is seeking feedback from the industry on a draft circular that would revise rules on sala...

top_stories.title: BSP looks to remove salary loan tenor cap for flexible payments
top_stories.summary: THE BANGKO SENTRAL ng Pilipinas (BSP) is looking to remove limits on tenors of salary-based loans and instead allow financial institutions to decide on terms based on their assessment of borrowers’ capacity to pay. This comes amid calls from teachers asking for longer loan repayment terms to help ease their financia...

top_stories.title: BSP shifts from 5-year loan cap to risk-based lending framework
top_stories.summary: Previously capping loan terms at five years at most, the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) has moved to amend this rule, proposing a principles-based approach where loan length will be based on a comprehensive assessment of a borrower’s creditworthiness. top_stories.title: Barclays sees BSP pause first, then hike as war risks linger
top_stories.summary: British banking giant Barclays sees the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) holding key interest rates steady at this month’s policy meeting before possibly hiking in June, in a bid to support the already weak economic growth outlook even prior to the war in the Middle East. “With the ceasefire likely to raise hopes o...

top_stories.title: Philippine banks’ bad loans swelled to 6-month high in February
top_stories.summary: MANILA, Philippines — The share of bad loans to the total lending portfolio of Philippine banks rose to a six-month high in February, as sticky borrowing costs continued to weigh on debt servicing, especially among retail borrowers. At the same time, new data showed banks trimmed their allowance for credit losses to...
top_stories.why_it_matters: This matters because provisioning, NPLs, exposure, or stress signals reveal whether banks are protecting balance sheets or still leaning into growth.

secondary_signals.title: Banks’ bad loan ratio hits 6-month high in Feb.
secondary_signals.summary: THE PHILIPPINE BANKING sector’s gross nonperforming loan (NPL) ratio rose to a six-month high in February, preliminary data from the Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) showed. Domestic banks’ gross NPL ratio increased to 3.33% as of end-February from 3.31% a month earlier but eased from 3.38% a year ago. This was the...
secondary_signals.note: Useful supporting signal on banking-system behavior.

secondary_signals.title: BOP posts US$2.3 billion deficit in February 2026; End-February GIR rise to US$113.3 billion[1]
secondary_signals.summary: PDate: 3/19/2026 Content: ​The Philippines’ balance of payments (BOP) registered a deficit of US$2.3 billion in February 2026. For January to February 2026, the BOP recorded an overall deficit of US$2.7 billion. The gross international reserves (GIR) increased to US$113.3 billion as of end-February 2026. This level...

secondary_signals.title: War’s impact on borrowers’ capacity to pay loans flagged
secondary_signals.summary: THE Philippine banking system could see a higher proportion of bad loans to the total loan books as the Middle East conflict saps the purchasing power and capacity to pay of Filipino borrowers especially in fuel and cost-sensitive sectors.
secondary_signals.note: Useful supporting signal on banking-system behavior.

secondary_signals.title: Rules on salary-based loans to be liberalized
secondary_signals.summary: THE Bangko Sentral ng Pilipinas (BSP) expects its amendments to regulations governing salary‑based general‑purpose consumption loans would provide “greater repayment flexibility” to and help ease debt‑servicing burden of borrowers and salaried employees.
secondary_signals.note: Useful supporting signal on banking-system behavior.

secondary_signals.title: BSP, pinagunahan ang ikalawang Iftar para palakasin ang kooperasyon sa Islamic finance
secondary_signals.summary: PDate: 3/19/2026 Content: ​​Nagtipon ang mga stakeholder ng Islamic banking and finance para sa Iftar sa BSP head office sa Maynila noong 10 Marso 2025. secondary_signals.title: FCDU loans increased by 2.9% to US$15.56 billion in Q4 2025
secondary_signals.summary: PDate: 3/31/2026 Content: ​Foreign currency deposit unit (FCDU) loans rose to US$15.56 billion in the fourth quarter of 2025.
